sanskrit: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः। अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके॥
transliteration: adhaś cordhvaṃ prasṛtās tasya śākhā guṇa-pravṛddhā viṣaya-pravālāḥ | adhaś ca mūlāny anusantatāni karmānubandhīni manuṣya-loke ||
literal_meaning: Downward and upward spread its branches, strengthened by the gunas; the sense-objects are its twigs; and downward stretch the roots, binding a person through karma in the human world.
contextual_meaning: The mind’s map is a banyan whose branches grow wherever attention is watered by the three gunas. Every sense-object is a new twig; every rooted memory a karma-fiber that keeps the self entangled in the human story.
psychological_state: Trance-like absorption in an ever-branching narrative of experience, mistaking the tree of cognition for the ground of Being.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m.—each post a twig, each like a root-fiber—feeling simultaneously exhilarated and trapped, yet unable to close the app.
anchor_text: Notice how every thought you feed grows roots that hold you here.
